scope: Decision cards for the eight MVK core element IDs only. Context-only Elementor V4 documentation is tracked in the doc coverage index.

cards.short_summary: Choose Div Block for a light structural wrapper when layout behavior should stay simple.
use_when: A node is mainly a wrapper or grouping boundary.
One-dimensional or two-dimensional layout behavior is not the main decision.
avoid_when: Children need deliberate flex alignment or distribution.
A linked wrapper would contain clickable children.
decision_questions: Is this structure or layout?
Does the wrapper need semantic tag evidence?
Could Flexbox or Grid express the layout more honestly?
architect_responsibilities: Decide wrapper role and reject layout alternatives.
ce_responsibilities: Check constructability, containing-block needs, and clickable topology.
builder_responsibilities: Execute only the locked wrapper decision and do not create nested clickable topology.
responsive_responsibilities: Validate runtime wrapper behavior when layout depends on it.
common_failure_modes: Using Div Block as vague replacement for layout.
Adding a link to a wrapper that contains clickable children.

cards.short_summary: Choose Flexbox for one-dimensional layout decisions where direction, alignment, and distribution matter.
use_when: Children need row or column distribution.
Alignment, ordering, gap, or wrapping is central.
avoid_when: The node is only a lightweight wrapper.
The layout needs explicit rows and columns.
decision_questions: Is the layout primarily one-dimensional?
Which axis owns the decision?
What evidence proves Builder can emit the locked controls?
architect_responsibilities: Define layout intent and reject Grid when not two-dimensional.
ce_responsibilities: Verify layout controls, responsive implications, and nested clickable constraints.
builder_responsibilities: Use exact locked control values only.
responsive_responsibilities: Validate wrapping and alignment across breakpoints.
common_failure_modes: Using Flexbox for true grid layout.
Treating source docs as exact UI-path proof.

cards.short_summary: Choose Grid when the decision requires two-dimensional rows and columns rather than a single flex axis.
use_when: Rows and columns are both meaningful.
Placement must be reasoned as a two-axis layout.
avoid_when: A single row or column flow is enough.
Only a simple wrapper is required.
decision_questions: Is there real two-dimensional placement?
Is the source only Grid container evidence?
What remains unproven for the target project?
architect_responsibilities: Mark the Grid source limitation honestly.
ce_responsibilities: Verify target constructability and documented grid limits before Builder-ready.
builder_responsibilities: Do not substitute Flexbox without Architect repair.
common_failure_modes: Using Grid when Flexbox is sufficient.

cards.short_summary: Choose Heading for semantic title text that must remain editable, searchable, and structurally meaningful.
use_when: Text is a section title or heading.
Heading semantics matter beyond visual styling.
avoid_when: Text is flattened into an image or SVG for decoration.
decision_questions: Is this semantic heading content?
What heading level is intended?
Must the text remain editable?
architect_responsibilities: Separate semantic heading choice from visual styling.
builder_responsibilities: Do not flatten heading text into media.
common_failure_modes: Choosing Heading only for visual size.
Losing editable text by using media.

cards.short_summary: Choose Paragraph for body text that should remain editable and semantically separate from headings.
use_when: Text is body copy or description.
The content must remain editable and searchable.
avoid_when: The text is a heading or call-to-action.
decision_questions: Is this body text?
Should it be searchable and editable?
Is visual styling being confused with semantics?
architect_responsibilities: Separate paragraph semantics from visual styling.
ce_responsibilities: Verify text constructability and content boundaries.
responsive_responsibilities: Validate readable text behavior across viewports.
common_failure_modes: Flattening real text into image media.

cards.short_summary: Choose Button for primary call-to-action decisions where click topology and link/action evidence matter.
use_when: The node is a call to action or explicit click target.
Interaction topology can be proven safe.
avoid_when: It would create nested clickable topology.
decision_questions: Is the element itself interactive?
Is there a clickable ancestor or descendant?
Is the action URL or behavior proven?
ce_responsibilities: Verify action constructability and nested-link constraints.
responsive_responsibilities: Validate target size and runtime interaction.
common_failure_modes: Nested clickable button inside linked container.
Treating docs as proof of actual URL/action.

cards.short_summary: Choose Image for raster or media assets when source, alt/decorative treatment, and responsive scaling matter.
use_when: The asset is raster or photographic media.
Alt text or decorative treatment must be decided.
avoid_when: The asset is a scalable vector best represented as SVG.
The content is real editable text.
decision_questions: Is this raster media or vector artwork?
Is it meaningful or decorative?
Does link behavior create topology risk?
architect_responsibilities: Decide Image vs SVG and semantic treatment.
ce_responsibilities: Verify asset availability and responsive needs.
builder_responsibilities: Do not invent media source or link state.

cards.short_summary: Choose SVG for scalable vector graphics when the decision is media artwork, not editable text.
use_when: The asset is vector artwork or iconography.
Scaling should stay crisp and non-raster.
avoid_when: The content is real editable/searchable text.
decision_questions: Is the asset vector or raster?
Is it decorative or meaningful?
What proves source/provenance and responsive scaling?
architect_responsibilities: Decide SVG vs Image and semantic treatment.
builder_responsibilities: Do not substitute Image or invent the SVG source.
responsive_responsibilities: Validate rendered scaling and runtime behavior.
common_failure_modes: Assuming source docs prove project upload/security allowance.
